title: brachionostylum pullei
intro: Brachionostylum pullei, also known as the Pulley's brachionostylum or Pulley's toothache plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to New Guinea and is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as providing relief from toothache and other types of pain, as well as reducing inflammation and fever. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the Brachionostylum pullei plant are often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures that can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
